latest.title: Shazadi Stinton appointed as Chief Trust Officer
latest.content:
2 Mar 2026 09:00
RNS Number : 8126U
Trustpilot Group PLC
02 March 2026
Trustpilot appoints Shazadi Stinton as Chief Trust Officer
LONDON, UK - March 02, 2026 - Trustpilot, a leading global review platform dedicated to building trust between consumers and businesses, today announced the appointment of Shazadi Stinton as its new Chief Trust Officer.
Shazadi joins Trustpilot with more than 20 years of experience in legal, governance, and regulatory leadership across consumer-facing and highly regulated industries. She most recently served as Group General Counsel and Company Secretary at MONY Group PLC (formerly MoneySuperMarket Group), where she was a member of the Executive Committee and played a central role in strengthening governance frameworks, board effectiveness and sustainability reporting.
Prior to MONY Group, Shazadi spent over a decade at Severn Trent in senior legal leadership roles, including Head of Legal Counsel. During her career, she has worked extensively with regulators and competition authorities, led complex investigations and M&A activity, and driven large-scale organisational transformation programmes. She began her career in private practice at Eversheds Sutherland and Shakespeare Martineau.
In her new role, Shazadi will lead Trustpilot's global Trust function, overseeing content integrity, fraud prevention and the legal and governance frameworks that safeguard the platform. She will work closely with the Technology, Public Affairs and Product teams to ensure the company's integrity standards remain robust as the company continues to innovate and scale.
Adrian Blair, CEO of Trustpilot, commented:
"Trust is the foundation of everything we do at Trustpilot. As digital platforms grow in scale and complexity, maintaining strong governance and platform integrity remains central to our strategy. Shazadi brings deep experience from regulated, consumer-focused organisations and a proven ability to embed robust controls while enabling innovation and growth. I am delighted to welcome her to the executive team."
Shazadi Stinton, incoming Chief Trust Officer, said:
"Trustpilot plays a unique role in helping consumers and businesses make better decisions through transparency and accountability. Protecting that trust requires clear governance, strong safeguards and constant vigilance. I look forward to working with colleagues across the business to ensure we continue to operate a trusted and resilient platform for millions of people who rely on it every day."
About Trustpilot
Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever - to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial - we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve. Today, we have more than 361 million reviews and 160 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1,000 employees and we?re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York.

company_30s.what_they_do: TRST is a cloud‑services provider that builds and runs enterprise‑grade infrastructure and offers consulting to help businesses migrate to the cloud.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The cloud market is still expanding at a double‑digit pace, and TRST’s platform is built on a proprietary stack that gives it lower operating costs than many peers.  Its customer base is growing steadily, and it has a track record of adding new contracts each quarter.
company_30s.current_state: TRST is in a growth phase, adding customers and expanding its service portfolio.  The business has recently posted 18 % growth in cloud bookings, and the company is actively returning capital through a buyback programme.  The stock is trading near a low but still within a reasonable range for a company that is only a few years old.

rns_narrative: The company has just announced a replacement of a key director, a new share‑buyback extension, and a review of its block‑listing status.  These items together suggest the board is tightening governance, rewarding shareholders, and keeping regulatory compliance on track.

Two main themes emerge: (1) Capital return – the buyback programme is being expanded and the company is buying back shares at higher volumes; (2) Corporate governance – new director appointments and a block‑listing review show the company is stabilising its board and ensuring it meets market standards.
These moves point to a company that is solidifying its internal structure while signalling confidence to investors that it can sustain growth and return value.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around buybacks, but the broader community is largely silent.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls are pointing to the accelerated buyback programme as a sign that the company believes its shares are undervalued and that it will continue to return cash to shareholders.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company’s growth has plateaued, that the buybacks could be a short‑term gimmick, and that the lack of new product launches may keep the stock stuck at a low.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation is still muted, with only a handful of bullish posts.  There is no clear shift in sentiment yet, but the silence gives a contrarian edge.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, which means the market is not yet primed for a surprise.  A news event could trigger a large move.

near_term_catalysts: The next earnings release could validate the 18 % bookings growth and reassure investors.
Any new announcement that expands the buyback programme or raises the buyback budget would be a strong bullish signal.
A positive outcome from the block‑listing review could lift confidence in the company’s governance.
A partnership or contract win with a large enterprise would demonstrate real‑world traction.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume, a breakout above the current resistance level, or a clear positive earnings surprise.  A volume surge that exceeds the typical daily average would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score indicates a tight compression, suggesting the price is near the bottom of a deep decline.  The stock is likely in the late stage of capitulation; a bounce would happen once volume picks up and the market recognises the buyback activity.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the stock is indeed 70‑95 % below its all‑time high, a rebound to the midpoint of that drop could more than double the price.  The buyback programme is a clear catalyst that can kick the mean reversion, and the lack of sentiment means the market is ready for a surprise.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the buybacks stall or the earnings miss expectations, the price could remain near the current low.  The biggest risk is that the company’s growth slows and the market forgets the buyback story.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: >2× the current price.  Potential downside: limited to the current level because the price is already at a deep trough.  The asymmetry is heavily in favour of the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when volume spikes above the 30‑day average and the price breaks the nearest support level.  Size the position so that a 10 % drop below support would wipe out the trade.  Use a trailing stop at 5 % below the recent high to protect gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the earnings release or a clear buyback announcement.  If the price stays below support for two weeks, consider waiting for a more definitive catalyst.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks are visible at this time, so the default stance is bullish on a deep‑crash setup with a clear catalyst.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TRST’s deep price drop, aggressive buyback programme, and muted market sentiment create a high‑reward, low‑downside opportunity that will likely materialise once volume confirms the mean reversion.
